fullDefinition: as it were, so to speak, effectively, essentially; used to mark a description as figurative, simplified or otherwise not to be taken as absolute, but illustrative of an important point

explanation: quälen — to torture, to torment, to agonize. Also: to cause sorrow, to upset, to worry, to harass, to bother, to toil, to struggle
ratsam — advisable.
recherchieren — to research, to investigate.
rechtlich — legal. Also: lawful
reduzieren — to reduce.
religiös — religious (concerning religion). Also: religious (committed to the practice of religion)
fullDefinition: religious (concerning religion)

explanation: renommiert — renowned.
revolutionieren — to revolutionize.

statement: runter means "down, down here" in German
explanation: runter — down, down here, downstairs (towards the speaker). Also: down, down there, downstairs (away from the speaker)
fullDefinition: down, down here, downstairs (towards the speaker)

explanation: Rätoromanisch — Rhaeto-Romance (also referred to as die rätoromanische Sprache or die rätoromanischen Sprachen). Also: Romansch
fullDefinition: Rhaeto-Romance (also referred to as die rätoromanische Sprache or die rätoromanischen Sprachen)

explanation: sanft — gentle.
satt — not hungry, satiated, full, done. Also: fed up, sick of (see usage notes), full, ample

explanation: schmal — narrow, slender (not wide, comparatively long), small. Also: meagre, scarce

explanation: schweben — to hover, soar, float, drift.

schwingen — to swing (to move back and forth). Also: to move (somewhere) by swinging, to swing (to move something using a swinging motion), to wave; to brandish, to vibrate, to oscillate, to resound, to propagate (somewhere) as sound, to move throughout (an area) resounding, to be expressed (in someone's words), to ski back and forth, to arc, to clean (hemp or flax) of wood residues, to wrestle in the Schwingen (Swiss wrestling) style
fullDefinition: to swing (to move back and forth)

explanation: sehnen — to yearn, to long.
seither — since then.
seltsam — strange, weird, odd, funny, curious. Also: rare, seldom
senken — to lower.
seriös — reputable, respectable, dignified; not shady or dubious; appearing civil, reliable, legitimate. Also: giving such an impression, thus formal, elegant
fullDefinition: reputable, respectable, dignified; not shady or dubious; appearing civil, reliable, legitimate

explanation: sichtbar — visible.
signalisieren — to signal.
sinnlos — meaningless, pointless, senseless. Also: excessive, disproportionate
slawisch — Slavic, Slav, Slavonic.
Sorbisch — Sorbian (belonging or relating to the Sorbian people). Also: Sorbian (in or relating to the Sorbian language)
fullDefinition: Sorbian (belonging or relating to the Sorbian people)

statement: sozusagen means "so to speak" in German
explanation: sozusagen — so to speak, in a manner of speaking.

staatlich — state, government. Also: state-owned
statistisch — statistical.
stattdessen — instead, in lieu (of).

steigern — to increase, to raise. Also: to boost, to improve, to enhance, to compare
stetig — steady, continuous (without break, cessation, or interruption in time).
fullDefinition: steady, continuous (without break, cessation, or interruption in time)

Also: to govern, to direct, to head; to control, [with auf (+ accusative)] to use something or someone for support, to pay a tax, to levy from, to tax, [with dative] to contravene, to withsit, [with dative] to assist, to come to the aid of

explanation: tagelang — lasting for days.

statement: tagsüber means "during the day" in German
explanation: tagsüber — by day, during the day, in the day, in the daytime.
fullDefinition: by day, during the day, in the day, in the daytime

explanation: technisch — technical. Also: technological, engineering
teils — partly, partially.

statement: thematisieren means "to bring up" in German

theoretisch — theoretical.

umweltfreundlich — environmentally friendly.
unbemerkt — unnoticed, unseen, unobserved.
unbezahlt — unpaid.

explanation: unentgeltlich — not in return for payment. Also: not to purchase, not for valuable consideration
ungehindert — unhindered. Also: unimpeded
ungern — unwillingly; usually expressed in English through verb phrases such as "doesn't like", "don't want", etc..

explanation: unklar — unclear.

unzählig — countless, innumerable.

verleihen — to award (someone an honor, a medal, etc); to confer (a title or degree upon someone). Also: to lend (something to someone, for a finite period of time), to vest (a power in someone), to give (a right to someone), to give (an attribute to something)
fullDefinition: to award (someone an honor, a medal, etc); to confer (a title or degree upon someone)

explanation: vernünftig — reasonable, sensible.

verpflichten — to oblige someone, make someone promise. Also: to commit, to oblige, to oblige, to swear, to be sworn, to engage, to bind someone by contract, to oblige someone, to be an obligation, to undertake (to commit oneself to an obligation)

versenden — to ship, to dispatch.
verstorben — deceased, late.
verstärken — to reinforce. Also: to amplify, to penetrate, to grow stronger
vertiefen — to deepen. Also: to delve
verzweifelt — desperate.
verändert — changed, altered.
verärgern — to annoy, to anger, to upset. Also: to antagonize
veröffentlicht — published.
vielfach — multiple, manifold.
vielfältig — diverse, varied. Also: manifold

explanation: vollkommen — perfect (fitting its definition precisely). Also: complete
fullDefinition: perfect (fitting its definition precisely)

explanation: vorhanden — available, existing.
vorkommen — to occur, happen. Also: to seem, appear, to appear, to occur (in a given location or situation), to feel (smart, stupid, clever, dumb, etc.)
vorlegen — to present, to submit. Also: to serve (food) to put down, put out (cat food etc.), to put on (a chain, a latch), to put (a bolt) across, to advance

vorweisen — to show. Also: to perform

explanation: zunehmend — increasing, growing, mounting.

statement: zurechtfinden means "to find one's way" in German

statement: zurückführen means "to trace back" in German

explanation: zurücktreten — to step back. Also: to resign, to step down, to rescind, to cancel
zusagen — to accept, to promise to come. Also: to pledge, to promise, to please; to appeal to

statement: zuschauen means "to look on" in German
explanation: zuschauen — to look on. Also: to watch

explanation: Änderung — change, modification.
Äußerung — utterance. Also: statement
Öffentlichkeitsarbeit — public relations (profession). Also: outreach

explanation: Öffnung — opening. Also: orifice
Übernachtung — overnight stay (at a hotel etc.). Also: sleepover

explanation: Überschrift — heading. Also: headline
Überschwemmung — flooding; flood; overflow of a body of water.

Also: to translate, to broadcast, televise, to hand over something to someone, entrust someone with something, confer a right on someone, to have a post-term birth, to be passed on to someone (of a disease or illness), to communicate itself to someone (of enthusiasm, nervousness, etc.)
